name: Smith Machine Close-Grip Bench Press
resistance_profile: The fixed vertical bar path lets you keep the elbows tucked and bias the triceps through the press, with the triceps working hardest from the mid-range through lockout.
cues: Keep the elbows tucked, not flared
Lower to the lower chest, not the neck
execution_steps: Set a flat bench under the Smith bar and grip slightly narrower than shoulder width.
Unrack the bar and hold it over the lower chest with the elbows tucked toward the ribs.
Lower the bar to the lower chest, keeping the elbows close to the body.
Press straight up along the fixed path to lockout, driving through the triceps.
good_when: You want to overload the triceps with a press but keep the bar path stable
Training alone and want the safety of the Smith catches
You struggle to groove a straight bar path with a free barbell
bad_when: The fixed path irritates your wrists, elbows or shoulders
You want the stabiliser demand of a free barbell
common_errors: Gripping too narrow and straining the wrists
Flaring the elbows and shifting load to the shoulders
Bouncing the bar off the chest
progressions: Pause on the chest before pressing
regressions: Use a machine chest press or close-grip push-up
